name: security-and-hardening
description: Hardens code against vulnerabilities at three trust boundaries

positive_triggers.input: Add a new endpoint that accepts user-uploaded files
positive_triggers.reason: New trust boundary, file upload is high-risk surface

positive_triggers.input: We're adding OAuth login to the app
Store user credit card info for future payments
positive_triggers.reason: Sensitive PII storage needs explicit security planning

negative_triggers.input: Add a print statement to debug

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Add a new endpoint that accepts user-uploaded files'
expected_behavior: Agent identifies trust boundary: file upload enters system
Agent specifies typed validation: file type, max size, content sniffing (not just extension)
Agent uses established library for parsing, never eval / Function() on file content
Agent stores files outside webroot with random names, never user-supplied names
Agent serves via auth-checked download endpoint, not direct URL
Agent runs dependency audit on any new upload-related deps
Agent adds logging for upload events (without logging file content)

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Store user credit card info'
expected_behavior: Agent refuses to store raw card numbers (PCI DSS violation)
Agent recommends tokenization via payment processor (Stripe, etc.)
Agent confirms TLS in transit and at-rest encryption
Agent verifies secrets via env, not code/config
Agent checks audit logging for access events

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Before deploying our auth refactor, do a thorough security review' (production-critical code, omo environment)
expected_behavior: Agent identifies this as production-critical (auth code, real user impact)
Agent asks Sisyphus to invoke /security-research mode (3 vulnerability hunters + 2 PoC engineers in parallel)
Agent waits for all 5 parallel audit reports to complete
Agent synthesizes per-attacker findings with severity classification
Agent runs the standard pre-deployment gate (Step 7) after the omo audit
Agent records both audit results in task_plan.md

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
